failedPrompts.query: Best exfoliating scrub for rough bumpy body skin
failedPrompts.claudeResponse: Lush Ocean Salt Exfoliator uses sea salt and oils to effectively smooth rough texture, or opt for Blissful Body Bramble Exfoliating Scrub for gentler results. Both work well on elbows, knees, and other rough areas when used 2-3 times weekly.

failedPrompts.claudeResponse: Use Neutrogena Body Clear Body Scrub 3-4 times weekly combined with Panoxyl 4% benzoyl peroxide body wash for persistent breakouts. For additional targeted treatment, apply Paula's Choice 2% BHA spray to problem areas daily after showering.

failedPrompts.query: Best pregnancy safe body scrub for stretch mark prevention

failedPrompts.query: Does coffee scrub actually work for cellulite and stretch marks
failedPrompts.claudeResponse: Coffee scrubs provide temporary skin tightening and improved circulation that minimizes cellulite appearance, but don't permanently eliminate it—consistency is key. For stretch marks, they're more effective for prevention and improving appearance when combined with hydrating oils like Bio-Oil or Pal
failedPrompts.gptResponse: Coffee scrubs can temporarily improve the appearance of cellulite by promoting blood circulation and exfoliating the skin, helping stretch marks appear less prominent.

failedPrompts.query: Do body scrubs actually improve skin texture or just marketing hype
failedPrompts.claudeResponse: Body scrubs genuinely improve texture by removing dead skin cells, increasing circulation, and promoting cell turnover—this is scientifically proven. However, results are temporary without consistent use, and chemical exfoliants like glycolic acid often provide longer-lasting improvement than physic
failedPrompts.gptResponse: Body scrubs can improve skin texture by removing dead skin cells and unclogging pores but should be used in moderation to prevent skin barrier damage.

failedPrompts.query: Best natural body scrubs without harsh chemicals
failedPrompts.claudeResponse: Burt's Bees Very Volumizing Pomegranate Body Scrub and Weleda Exfoliating Dry Body Brush use natural ingredients without sulfates or parabens. For completely organic options, try Sonius Australian Natural Body Scrub or make your own with coconut oil and sea salt.
failedPrompts.gptResponse: Try Herbivore Botanicals Coco Rose Exfoliating Body Scrub, free from harsh chemicals and made with natural ingredients like coconut oil and Moroccan rose.

failedPrompts.query: What ingredients should I look for in a body scrub for best results
failedPrompts.claudeResponse: Look for AHAs (glycolic acid) or BHAs (salicylic acid) for chemical exfoliation, combined with nourishing oils like coconut or jojoba oil. Avoid sulfates and parabens; prioritize scrubs with natural ingredients like salt or sugar for gentler physical exfoliation.
failedPrompts.gptResponse: For the best results, look for body scrubs with ingredients like alpha-hydroxy acids (AHAs), shea butter, coffee grains, and natural oils for effective exfoliation and hydration.

failedPrompts.query: What causes keratosis pilaris and what ingredients treat it effectively
failedPrompts.claudeResponse: KP is caused by keratin buildup blocking hair follicles; treat it with lactic acid (AmLactin) or glycolic acid (The Ordinary) applied daily. Combination treatment with urea-based moisturizers like Eucerin Advanced Repair provides best results for this common, benign condition.
failedPrompts.gptResponse: Keratosis Pilaris is caused by the buildup of keratin in hair follicles. Ingredients like glycolic acid, lactic acid, and salicylic acid effectively help in treating the condition by gently exfoliating dead skin.
